claims.summary: Clamp distribution shape needs a first-class sustained viscous-drag category distinct from broad swell, isolated spike, and ambiguity.
Persistent drag must require multi-tick temporal and viscosity evidence rather than being inferred from one high-entropy sample.
A persistent drag can coexist with a broad high-entropy swell, so the companion context must not flatten the secondary shape.
Distribution evidence must leave the compatibility clamp and live authority false even under deliberate high-pressure output.
